---
title: "Figures for the manuscript"
author: "Ziang Zhang"
date: "2025-12-01"
output: workflowr::wflow_html
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(patchwork)
library(cowplot)
library(fastTopics)
library(dplyr)
library(pheatmap)
library(scales)
library(scattermore)

data_path <- "../data/"
code_path <- "../code/"
data_path <- "data/"
code_path <- "code/"
source(paste0(code_path, "ROC.R"))
# seurat_meta <- readRDS(paste0(data_path, "seurat_meta.rds"))
seurat_meta <- readRDS(paste0(data_path, "igt1_96_withtotalvi20260206_clean_ADTonly.Rds"))@meta.data
flashier_snmf_summary <- readRDS(paste0(data_path, "flashier_snmf_summary.rds"))
cells_flashier <- rownames(flashier_snmf_summary$L_pm)
cells_seurat <- seurat_meta$cellID
which(!cells_flashier %in% cells_seurat)
L_pm <- flashier_snmf_summary$L_pm[cells_flashier %in% cells_seurat, ]

filter_cells_by_total_membership <- function (L, max_val = 10, numiter = 10) {
  n <- nrow(L)
  rows <- 1:n
  for (iter in 1:numiter) {
    x <- rowSums(L)
    cat(sprintf("%d. Filtered out %d cells.\n",iter,sum(x > max_val)))
    i <- which(x <= max_val)
    L <- L[i,]
    rows <- rows[i]
    d <- apply(L,2,max)
    L <- scale_cols(L,1/d)
  }
  return(rows)
}
scale_cols <- function (A, b) t(t(A) * b)
```

Normalize L_pm for better visualization, so max of each column is 1

```{r}
D <- apply(L_pm,2,max)
L_pm_norm <- scale_cols(L_pm,1/D)
F_pm <- flashier_snmf_summary$F_pm
F_pm_norm <- scale_cols(F_pm,D)
```

```{r}
colnames(L_pm) <- paste0("K", seq_len(ncol(L_pm)))
colnames(F_pm) <- paste0("F", seq_len(ncol(F_pm)))

D <- diag(1 / apply(L_pm, 2, function(x) max(x)))
L <- L_pm %*% D
cells <- filter_cells_by_total_membership(L,numiter = 12)
seurat_meta_filtered <- seurat_meta[cells,]
L_pm_filtered <- L_pm[cells,]
d <- apply(L_pm_filtered,2,max)
L_pm_filtered <- scale_cols(L_pm_filtered,1/d)

# normalize F correspondingly
F_pm_filtered <- scale_cols(F_pm,d)

L_pm_no_thymocytes_normalized <- L_pm_filtered[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1 != "thymocyte", ]
seurat_meta_filtered_no_thymocytes <- seurat_meta_filtered[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1 != "thymocyte", ]
```

# Figure 1: 

## (a) Illustration of EBMF

Create using illustrator.


## (b) Number of active cells in each GP

For each GP, we define its number of active cells as the number of cells with loading > 0.1 of the max loading in that GP.:

```{r}
options(scipen = 999)
L_pm_norm_col <- L_pm_filtered / matrix(apply(L_pm_filtered, 2, function(x) max(x)), nrow = nrow(L_pm_filtered), ncol = ncol(L_pm_filtered), byrow = TRUE)

# a cell is active if its loading is larger than 0.1 of the max value in that GP
gp_active_cell_counts <- colSums((L_pm_norm_col) > 1e-1)

pdf("figures/Figure1/Figure1b_hist_active_cells_per_GP.pdf", width = 6, height = 4, useDingbats = FALSE)
hist(gp_active_cell_counts, 
     breaks = 100, 
     xlab = "Number of highly active cells per GP",
     main = "Histogram of highly active cells per GP", freq = T)
dev.off()
```


Take a look at number of active programs versus activated status

```{r}
cells_activated <- seurat_meta_filtered$cellID[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2_group == "activated"]

L_pm_activated <- L_pm_filtered[seurat_meta_filtered$cellID %in% cells_activated, ]

gp_active_cell_counts_activated <- rowSums(L_pm_activated  > 1e-1)

cells_resting <- seurat_meta_filtered$cellID[seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level2_group == "resting"]

L_pm_resting <- L_pm_filtered[seurat_meta_filtered$cellID %in% cells_resting, ]

gp_active_cell_counts_resting <- rowSums(L_pm_resting  > 1e-1)

# plot the boxplot comparing the number of active cells in activated vs resting groups
# boxplot(gp_active_cell_counts_resting)
# boxplot(gp_active_cell_counts_activated)
# side by side boxplot
gp_active_cell_counts_df <- data.frame(
  Group = c(rep("Activated", length(gp_active_cell_counts_activated)), rep("Resting", length(gp_active_cell_counts_resting))),
  Active_Cell_Counts = c(gp_active_cell_counts_activated, gp_active_cell_counts_resting)
)

p <- ggplot(gp_active_cell_counts_df, aes(x = Group, y = Active_Cell_Counts, fill = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.4, width = 0.6, alpha = 0.8, color = "gray40") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Activated" = "#1f78b4", "Resting" = "#e31a1c")) +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "",
    y = "Number of highly active GPs"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/Figure1/Figure1b_boxplot_active_cells_per_GP.pdf",
  plot = p, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 300
)
```

Similar boxplot comparing across level 1 annotations

```{r}
gp_active_cell_counts_level1 <- data.frame(
  Group = seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1,
  Active_Cell_Counts = rowSums(L_pm_filtered > 1e-1)
)

p <- ggplot(gp_active_cell_counts_level1, aes(x = Group, y = Active_Cell_Counts, fill = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.4, width = 0.6, alpha = 0.8, color = "gray40") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ZemmourLib::immgent_colors$level1) +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "",
    y = "Number of highly active GPs"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/Figure1/Figure1b_boxplot_active_cells_per_GP_level1.pdf",
  plot = p, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 300
)

```

```{r}
# load the protein matrix protein_mat_normalized_lognorm
protein_mat_normalized_lognorm <- readRDS(paste0(data_path, "protein_mat_normalized_lognorm.rds"))
protein_mat_normalized_lognorm <- protein_mat_normalized_lognorm[rownames(L_pm_filtered) , "CD44"]
```

```{r}
# plot scatter plot of CD44 protein level vs number of active GPs (defined as loading > 0.1 of max loading in that GP)
gp_active_cell_counts <- rowSums(L_pm_filtered > 1e-1)
gp_active_cell_counts_df <- data.frame(
  CD44_Protein_Level = protein_mat_normalized_lognorm,
  Active_GP_Counts = gp_active_cell_counts
)

# take a random sample of 10000 points for better visualization
set.seed(123)
df_nz <- gp_active_cell_counts_df %>% dplyr::filter(CD44_Protein_Level > 0)
df_nz <- df_nz %>% sample_n(min(10000, nrow(df_nz)))
R <- cor(df_nz$CD44_Protein_Level, df_nz$Active_GP_Counts, use = "complete.obs")

p <- ggplot(df_nz, aes(CD44_Protein_Level, Active_GP_Counts)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1, size = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
   labs(
    title = "CD44 Protein Level vs Number of Active GPs",
    x = "CD44 Protein Level (log-normalized)",
    y = "Number of Active GPs"
  ) +
  annotate("text",
           x = min(df_nz$CD44_Protein_Level, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.5,
           y = max(df_nz$Active_GP_Counts, na.rm = TRUE) - 1,
           label = paste0("R = ", round(R, 2)),
           size = 4) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)


ggsave(
  filename = "figures/Figure1/Figure1b_scatterplot_CD44_vs_active_GPs.pdf",
  plot = p, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 300
)
```

```{r}
# also plot CD44 agains total loading across all GPs
total_loading <- rowSums(L_pm_filtered)
gp_total_loading_df <- data.frame(
  CD44_Protein_Level = protein_mat_normalized_lognorm,
  Total_Loading = total_loading
)
p <- ggplot(gp_total_loading_df, aes(x = CD44_Protein_Level, y = Total_Loading)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1, size = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "CD44 Protein Level vs Total Loading Across All GPs",
    x = "CD44 Protein Level (log-normalized)",
    y = "Total Loading Across All GPs"
  ) +
  # add regression line and correlation coefficient
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "blue") +
  # print correlation coefficient
  geom_text(
    data = data.frame(
      x = min(gp_total_loading_df$CD44_Protein_Level, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.5,
      y = max(gp_total_loading_df$Total_Loading, na.rm = TRUE) - 1,
      label = paste0("R = ", round(cor(gp_total_loading_df$CD44_Protein_Level, gp_total_loading_df$Total_Loading, use = "complete.obs"), 2))
    ),
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = label),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 4
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```




## (c) Number of genes active in each GP

```{r}
# divide each column by its max abs value
F_pm_norm_col <- F_pm / matrix(apply(F_pm, 2, function(x) max(abs(x))), nrow = nrow(F_pm), ncol = ncol(F_pm), byrow = TRUE)
# a gene is active if its abs factor value is larger than 0.25 of the max value in that GP
gp_active_gene_counts <- colSums(abs(F_pm_norm_col) > 0.25)
pdf("figures/Figure1/Figure1c_hist_active_genes_per_GP.pdf", width = 6, height = 4)
hist(gp_active_gene_counts, 
     breaks = 100, 
     xlab = "Number of highly active genes per GP", 
     main = "Histogram of highly active genes per GP", freq = T)
dev.off()
```




## (d) PVE in each GP

```{r}
pve_df <- data.frame(
  GP_Index = seq_along(pve_vec),
  PVE = pve_vec
)
p <- ggplot(pve_df, aes(x = GP_Index, y = PVE)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1e-4, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "PVE of each GP",
    x = "GP Index",
    y = "PVE"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
ggsave(
  filename = "figures/Figure1/Figure1d_elbow_plot_PVE_per_GP.pdf",
  plot = p, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 300
)
```




## (e) scatterplot

```{r}
level_1_AUC_list <- readRDS(paste0(data_path, "level_1_AUC_list_figure.rds"))
level_1_AUC <- level_1_AUC_list$auc
level_1_AUC_max <- apply(level_1_AUC, 2, max)
level_1_AUC_max_name <- apply(level_1_AUC, 2, function(x) rownames(level_1_AUC)[which.max(x)])
o <- order(level_1_AUC_max, decreasing = TRUE)
table_level_1_AUC <- data.frame(
  Factor = colnames(level_1_AUC)[o],
  Max_AUC = level_1_AUC_max[o],
  Annotation = level_1_AUC_max_name[o]
)

organ_AUC_list <- readRDS(paste0(data_path, "organ_simplified_AUC_list_figure.rds"))
organ_AUC <- organ_AUC_list$auc
# remove categories with small count
organ_small_count <- table(seurat_meta_filtered_no_thymocytes$organ_simplified)
organ_small_count <- names(organ_small_count[organ_small_count < 3000])
organ_AUC <- organ_AUC[!rownames(organ_AUC) %in% organ_small_count, ]
organ_AUC_max <- apply(organ_AUC, 2, max)
organ_AUC_max_name <- apply(organ_AUC, 2, function(x) rownames(organ_AUC)[which.max(x)])
o <- order(organ_AUC_max, decreasing = TRUE)
table_organ_AUC <- data.frame(
  Max_AUC = organ_AUC_max[o],
  Annotation = organ_AUC_max_name[o]
)

# merge the two AUC tables by factor
max_AUC_df <- data.frame(
  Factor = table_level_1_AUC$Factor,
  annotation_Level1 = table_level_1_AUC$Annotation,
  annotation_Organ = organ_AUC_max_name[match(table_level_1_AUC$Factor, names(organ_AUC_max))],
  Max_AUC_Organ = organ_AUC_max[match(table_level_1_AUC$Factor, names(organ_AUC_max))],
  Max_AUC_Level1 = table_level_1_AUC$Max_AUC
)
```

```{r}
df <- max_AUC_df %>%
  mutate(
    residual = Max_AUC_Level1 - Max_AUC_Organ,
    abs_res  = abs(residual)
  )

label_above <- df %>%
  filter(is.finite(residual), residual > 0, Max_AUC_Level1 > 0.85) %>%
  mutate(nudge_x = -0.035)

label_below <- df %>%
  filter(is.finite(residual), residual < 0, Max_AUC_Organ > 0.85) %>%
  mutate(nudge_x = 0.035)

base_plot <- ggplot(df, aes(Max_AUC_Organ, Max_AUC_Level1)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 1.8) +
  geom_point(data = label_above, color = "#1f78b4", alpha = 0.8, size = 1.8) +
  geom_point(data = label_below, color = "#e31a1c", alpha = 0.8, size = 1.8) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.46, 1.04), ylim = c(0.5, 1.02), expand = FALSE) +
  labs(
    x = "Max AUC (Organ Simplified)",
    y = "Max AUC (Level-1)",
    title = "Max AUC: Organ vs Level-1",
    subtitle = sprintf("Annotating top %d above (blue) and top %d below (red) y = x", top_n, top_n)
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

p_all <- base_plot +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = label_above,
    aes(label = paste0(Factor, ": ", annotation_Level1)),
    color = "#1f78b4",
    direction = "y",
    nudge_x = label_above$nudge_x,
    segment.color = "#1f78b4",
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.008, "npc"), type = "closed", angle = 20),  # smaller arrow
    force = 1.2, force_pull = 0.2,
    box.padding = 0.35, point.padding = 0.15,
    max.time = 2, max.iter = 5e3,
    min.segment.length = 0.01,
    segment.alpha = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = label_below,
    aes(label = paste0(Factor, ": ", annotation_Organ)),
    color = "#e31a1c",
    direction = "y",
    nudge_x = label_below$nudge_x,
    segment.color = "#e31a1c",
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.008, "npc"), type = "closed", angle = 20),  # smaller arrow
    force = 1.2, force_pull = 0.2,
    box.padding = 0.35, point.padding = 0.15,
    max.time = 2, max.iter = 5e3,
    min.segment.length = 0.01,
    segment.alpha = 0.7
  )

p_all

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/Figure1/Figure1e_scatterplot.pdf",
  plot = p_all, width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 300
)
```


## (e) boxplot

```{r}
highlights <- c("K22","K30","K58","K68")
color_coding <- ZemmourLib::immgent_colors$level1
Level1_match <- c("DN", "nonconv", "CD8", "Treg")
plot_factor_boxplot_level1 <- function(factor_id, 
                                L_pm_filtered, 
                                seurat_meta_filtered,
                                outline = FALSE,
                                log = FALSE,
                                color_coding) {
  df <- data.frame(
    Loading = L_pm_filtered[, factor_id],
    CellType = seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1
  )
  
  median_order <- df %>%
    group_by(CellType) %>%
    summarize(med = median(Loading, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    arrange(desc(med)) %>%        
    pull(CellType)
  
  df$CellType <- factor(df$CellType, levels = median_order)
  
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = CellType, y = Loading, fill = CellType)) +
    geom_boxplot(outliers = outline, outlier.size = 0.4, width = 0.6, alpha = 0.8, color = "gray40") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_coding) +
    # scale_y_log10() +
    labs(
      title = "",
      x = "",
      y = paste0("Loading K", factor_id)
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13, hjust = 0.5),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "none",
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
    )
  
  if (log) {
    p <- p + scale_y_log10()
  }
  
  return(p)
}

p1 <- plot_factor_boxplot_level1(factor_id = 68, L_pm_filtered = L_pm_filtered, seurat_meta_filtered = seurat_meta_filtered, color_coding = color_coding)
p2 <- plot_factor_boxplot_level1(factor_id = 58, L_pm_filtered = L_pm_filtered, seurat_meta_filtered = seurat_meta_filtered, color_coding = color_coding)
p3 <- plot_factor_boxplot_level1(factor_id = 30, L_pm_filtered = L_pm_filtered, seurat_meta_filtered = seurat_meta_filtered, color_coding = color_coding)
p4 <- plot_factor_boxplot_level1(factor_id = 22, L_pm_filtered = L_pm_filtered, seurat_meta_filtered = seurat_meta_filtered, color_coding = color_coding)
p_combined <- gridExtra::grid.arrange(p1, p2, p3, p4, ncol = 2)
ggsave(filename = "figures/Figure1/Figure1e_Boxplot.pdf",
       plot = p_combined,
       width = 8, height = 8)
```

## (e) structure plot

```{r}
match_category_to_factor <- function(auc_matrix) {
  n_factors    <- ncol(auc_matrix)
  n_categories <- nrow(auc_matrix)

  category_to_factor <- rep(NA_character_, n_categories)
  names(category_to_factor) <- rownames(auc_matrix)

  factor_assigned <- rep(FALSE, n_factors)
  names(factor_assigned) <- colnames(auc_matrix)

  category_score <- apply(auc_matrix, 1, max)    
  category_order <- order(category_score, decreasing = TRUE)
  ordered_categories <- rownames(auc_matrix)[category_order]

  for (cat in ordered_categories) {
    aucs <- auc_matrix[cat, ]

    aucs[factor_assigned] <- -Inf

    best_factor_idx <- which.max(aucs)
    best_factor_name <- names(aucs)[best_factor_idx]

    category_to_factor[cat] <- best_factor_name
    factor_assigned[best_factor_idx] <- TRUE
  }

  return(category_to_factor)
}
level1_category_to_factor <- match_category_to_factor(level_1_AUC)
color_coding <- ZemmourLib::immgent_colors$level1
colnames(L_pm_filtered) <- paste0("K", seq_len(ncol(L_pm_filtered)))
```

```{r}
set.seed(123)
color_coding <- ZemmourLib::immgent_colors$level1
# create level1_category_to_factor manually now
level1_category_to_factor <- c(
  "Treg" = "K68",
  "CD8" = "K58",
  "nonconv" = "K30",
  "DN" = "K22",
  "CD8aa" = "K29",
  "gdT" = "K3",
  "DP" = "K171"
)
fit2 <- L_pm_no_thymocytes_normalized[, level1_category_to_factor, drop = FALSE]
cell_type <- seurat_meta[rownames(fit2), "annotation_level1"]
cells <- which(cell_type == "CD4" | cell_type == "CD8")
cells <- sample(cells,1e5)
cells <- sort(c(cells,which(cell_type != "CD4" & cell_type != "CD8")))

structure_plot(fit2[cells,],gap = 20,n = 5000,
               colors = color_coding[names(level1_category_to_factor)],
               grouping = cell_type[cells]) +
  labs(y = "membership",color = "",fill = "") +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(nrow = 1),
    color = guide_legend(nrow = 1)
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.box = "horizontal",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 13)
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/Figure1/Figure1e_Structure_Plot.pdf",
  width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300
)
```

## (e) protein heatmap

```{r}
protein_flash_summary_lognorm <- readRDS(file = paste0(data_path, "protein_flash_selected_summary_lognorm.rds"))
# normalize by the max(abs) of each column
D_lognorm <- diag(1 / apply(protein_flash_summary_lognorm$F_pm, 2, function(x) max(abs(x))))
Protein_F_pm <- protein_flash_summary_lognorm$F_pm %*% D_lognorm
# colnames(Protein_F_pm) <- paste0("F", 1:ncol(flashier_snmf_summary$F_pm))[ flashier_snmf_summary$pve > 1e-4]
factor.info <- read.csv(file = paste0(data_path, "factors_comments_updated.xlsx - factor_pve.csv"), header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
factors_considered <- sort(factor.info$X[factor.info$strongly.replicated])
colnames(Protein_F_pm) <- paste0("F", factors_considered)
```

```{r}
kset <- paste0("F", c(68, 58, 30, 22))
Protein_F_pm_selected <- Protein_F_pm[, kset]
annotation_heatmap(Protein_F_pm_selected,n = 5,
                   dim = kset,
                   show_dims = kset,
                   compare_dims = kset,
                   select_features = "largest",
                   feature_sign = "both",
                   zero_value = 0.1,
                   font_size = 15) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "plain",size = 10))
ggsave(filename = "figures/Figure1/Figure1e_Protein_Heatmap.pdf",
       width = 6, height = 6)
```

## (f) dotplot

```{r}
organ_factors_selected <- c("K3", "K11", "K26", "K29", "K35", "K37", "K49", "K101")
organ_annotated <- c("small intestine epi", "placenta", "submandibular gland", "colon epi", "SLO", "mammary gland", "mammary gland", "thymus")

organ_df <- data.frame(
  Factor = organ_factors_selected,
  Annotation = organ_annotated
)

# compute the median loading for each level 1 annotation in each annotated organ
for (i in 1:nrow(organ_df)) {
  factor_id <- organ_df$Factor[i]

  selected_cells <- which(seurat_meta_filtered_no_thymocytes$organ_simplified == organ_df$Annotation[i])
  selected_L_pm_filtered <- L_pm_no_thymocytes_normalized[selected_cells, , drop = FALSE]
  selected_seurat_meta_filtered <- seurat_meta_filtered_no_thymocytes[selected_cells, , drop = FALSE]
  
  # avaliable level 1 annotations in the selected cells
  level1_annotations <- unique(selected_seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1)
  for (level1 in level1_annotations) {
    cells_level1 <- which(selected_seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1 == level1)
    median_loading <- median(selected_L_pm_filtered[cells_level1, factor_id], na.rm = TRUE)
    
    organ_df[i, level1] <- median_loading
  }
  
  
}
```

```{r}
organ_df_contrast <- data.frame(
  Annotation = organ_annotated
)
organ_df_contrast_vec <- c()
# compute the median loading for cells NOT in that organ
for (i in 1:nrow(organ_df)) {
  factor_id <- organ_df$Factor[i]
  selected_cells <- which(seurat_meta_filtered_no_thymocytes$organ_simplified != organ_df$Annotation[i])
  selected_L_pm_filtered <- L_pm_no_thymocytes_normalized[selected_cells, , drop = FALSE]
  
  organ_df_contrast_vec[i] <- median(selected_L_pm_filtered[, factor_id], na.rm = TRUE)
}
organ_df_contrast$median_contrast <- organ_df_contrast_vec
```


```{r}
organ_df_prop <- data.frame(
  Annotation = organ_annotated
)

# compute the proportion for each level 1 annotation in each annotated organ
for (i in 1:nrow(organ_df)) {
  selected_cells <- which(seurat_meta_filtered_no_thymocytes$organ_simplified == organ_df$Annotation[i])
  selected_seurat_meta_filtered <- seurat_meta_filtered_no_thymocytes[selected_cells, , drop = FALSE]
  
  # available level 1 annotations in the selected cells
  level1_annotations <- unique(selected_seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1)
  for (level1 in level1_annotations) {
    cells_level1 <- which(selected_seurat_meta_filtered$annotation_level1 == level1)
    organ_df_prop[i, level1] <- length(cells_level1) / nrow(selected_seurat_meta_filtered)
  }
}
```


```{r}
organ_df_diff <- organ_df

for (i in 1:nrow(organ_df_diff)) {
  contrast_value <- organ_df_contrast$median_contrast[i]
  level1_annotations <- colnames(organ_df_diff)[-(1:2)]
  
  for (level1 in level1_annotations) {
    x <- organ_df_diff[i, level1]
    
    if (contrast_value <= 0) {
      organ_df_diff[i, level1] <- NA
    } else {
      organ_df_diff[i, level1] <- log2(x / contrast_value)
    }
  }
}


level1_levels <- c("gdT", "CD8aa", "CD8", "nonconv", "DN", "Treg", "CD4", "DP")

prop_long <- organ_df_prop |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Annotation,
    names_to  = "level1",
    values_to = "prop"
  )

med_long <- organ_df_diff |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(level1_levels),
    names_to  = "level1",
    values_to = "median_loading"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Factor_label = paste0(Factor, "\n", Annotation),
    level1 = factor(level1, levels = level1_levels)
  )

plot_df <- med_long |>
  left_join(
    prop_long,
    by = c("Annotation", "level1")
  )

```

```{r}
p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Factor_label, y = level1)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = prop, color = median_loading), alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_y_discrete(name = "Level 1 annotation") +
  scale_x_discrete(name = "Factor (with organ simplified)") +
  scale_size_continuous(name  = "Proportion of cells\nin that organ", range = c(2, 10)) +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low       = "blue",
    mid       = "white",
    high      = "red",
    midpoint  = 0,
    limits   = c(-5, 5),
    oob      = squish,
    name      = "log2 fold-change\n(median loading)"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1,
      vjust = 1
    ),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

p

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/Figure1/Figure1f_Dotplot.pdf",
  plot = p,
  width = 8, height = 8
)

```



## (g) level2 scatterplot (healthy)

```{r}
factor_coef_list <- readRDS(file = paste0(data_path, "conditional_analysis_factor_organ_simplified.rds"))

max_coef_df <- data.frame(
  Factor = integer(),
  Max_Abs_Coefficient_Organ = numeric(),
  the_max_organ = character(),
  Max_Abs_Coefficient_Level2 = numeric(),
  the_max_level2 = character()
)

for (i in 1:length(factor_coef_list)) {
  coef_df <- factor_coef_list[[i]]
  max_abs_organ <- max(abs(coef_df$organ_coefs))
  max_abs_level2 <- max(abs(coef_df$level2_coefs))
  max_coef_df <- rbind(max_coef_df, data.frame(
    Factor = i,
    Max_Abs_Coefficient_Organ = max_abs_organ,
    the_max_organ = names(coef_df$organ_coefs)[which.max(abs(coef_df$organ_coefs))],
    Max_Abs_Coefficient_Level2 = max_abs_level2,
    the_max_level2 = names(coef_df$level2_coefs)[which.max(abs(coef_df$level2_coefs))]
  ))
  
  max_coef_df$the_max_organ[i] <- sub("^organ", "", max_coef_df$the_max_organ[i])
  max_coef_df$the_max_level2[i] <- sub("^level2", "", max_coef_df$the_max_level2[i])
}
```

```{r}
top_n <- 5  # how many points to annotate above and below y = x

df <- max_coef_df %>%
  mutate(
    residual = Max_Abs_Coefficient_Level2 - Max_Abs_Coefficient_Organ, 
    abs_res  = abs(residual)
  )

label_above <- df %>%
  filter(is.finite(residual), residual > 0) %>%
  slice_max(residual, n = top_n, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    label = paste0("K", Factor, "\n", the_max_level2)  
  )

label_below <- df %>%
  filter(is.finite(residual), residual < 0) %>%
  slice_min(residual, n = top_n, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    label = paste0("K", Factor, "\n", the_max_organ)   
  )

# --- Base plot ---
base_plot <- ggplot(df, aes(x = Max_Abs_Coefficient_Organ,
                            y = Max_Abs_Coefficient_Level2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 1.8) +
  geom_point(data = label_above, color = "#1f78b4", alpha = 0.6, size = 1.8) +
  geom_point(data = label_below, color = "#e31a1c", alpha = 0.6, size = 1.8) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0,
              linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 0.6), ylim = c(0, 0.6), expand = TRUE) +
  labs(
    x = "Max Absolute Coefficient (Organ Simplified)",
    y = "Max Absolute Coefficient (Level-2)",
    title = "Max Absolute Coefficient: Organ vs Level-2",
    subtitle = "healthy cells"
  )

p_all <- base_plot +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = label_above,
    aes(label = label),
    color = "#1f78b4", size = 3, max.overlaps = Inf,
    box.padding = 0.3, point.padding = 0.2, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = label_below,
    aes(label = label),
    color = "#e31a1c", size = 3, max.overlaps = Inf,
    box.padding = 0.3, point.padding = 0.2, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

p_all

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/Figure1/Figure1g_Level2_scatterplot_healthy.pdf",
  plot = p_all, width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 300
)
```


## (g) level2 scatterplot (all cells)
```{r}
factor_coef_list <- readRDS(file = paste0(data_path, "conditional_analysis_factor_condition_detailed.rds"))
```

```{r}
max_coef_df <- data.frame(
  Factor = integer(),
  Max_Abs_Coefficient_condition = numeric(),
  the_max_condition = character(),
  Max_Abs_Coefficient_Level2 = numeric(),
  the_max_level2 = character()
)

for (i in 1:length(factor_coef_list)) {
  coef_df <- factor_coef_list[[i]]
  max_abs_condition <- max(abs(coef_df$condition_coefs))
  max_abs_level2 <- max(abs(coef_df$level2_coefs))
  max_coef_df <- rbind(max_coef_df, data.frame(
    Factor = i,
    Max_Abs_Coefficient_condition = max_abs_condition,
    the_max_condition = names(coef_df$condition_coefs)[which.max(abs(coef_df$condition_coefs))],
    Max_Abs_Coefficient_Level2 = max_abs_level2,
    the_max_level2 = names(coef_df$level2_coefs)[which.max(abs(coef_df$level2_coefs))]
  ))
  
  max_coef_df$the_max_condition[i] <- sub("^condition", "", max_coef_df$the_max_condition[i])
  max_coef_df$the_max_level2[i] <- sub("^level2", "", max_coef_df$the_max_level2[i])
}
```

```{r}
top_n <- 5  # how many points to annotate above and below y = x
df <- max_coef_df %>%
  mutate(
    residual = Max_Abs_Coefficient_Level2 - Max_Abs_Coefficient_condition, 
    abs_res  = abs(residual)
  )
label_above <- df %>%
  filter(is.finite(residual), residual > 0) %>%
  slice_max(residual, n = top_n, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    label = paste0("K", Factor, "\n", the_max_level2)  
  )
label_below <- df %>%
  filter(is.finite(residual), residual < 0) %>%
  slice_min(residual, n = top_n, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    label = paste0("K", Factor, "\n", the_max_condition)   
  )
# --- Base plot ---
base_plot <- ggplot(df, aes(x = Max_Abs_Coefficient_condition,
                            y = Max_Abs_Coefficient_Level2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 1.8) +
  geom_point(data = label_above, color = "#1f78b4", alpha = 0.6, size = 1.8) +
  geom_point(data = label_below, color = "#e31a1c", alpha = 0.6, size = 1.8) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0,
              linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 0.9), ylim = c(0, 0.9), expand = TRUE) +
  labs(
    x = "Max Absolute Coefficient (Condition Detailed)",
    y = "Max Absolute Coefficient (Level-2)",
    title = "Max Absolute Coefficient: Condition vs Level-2",
    subtitle = "all cells"
  )
p_all <- base_plot +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = label_above,
    aes(label = label),
    color = "#1f78b4", size = 3, max.overlaps = Inf,
    box.padding = 0.3, point.padding = 0.2, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = label_below,
    aes(label = label),
    color = "#e31a1c", size = 3, max.overlaps = Inf,
    box.padding = 0.3, point.padding = 0.2, min.segment.length = 0
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

p_all

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/Figure1/Figure1g_Level2_scatterplot_all_cells.pdf",
  plot = p_all, width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 300
)
```




## (g) level2 structure plot

Within each level-1 category, we will produce a structure plot to visualize some highlighted factors across level-2 annotations.

```{r}
# load the AUC matrix for level2:
level_2_AUC_list <- readRDS(paste0(data_path, "level_2_within_level_1_AUC_list_figure.rds"))
level_2_AUC <- level_2_AUC_list$auc
# filter out annotations with less than 500 cells
which_less_500 <- sapply(rownames(level_2_AUC), function(x){
  sum(seurat_meta_filtered_no_thymocytes$annotation_level2 == x) < 500
})
level_2_AUC <- level_2_AUC[!which_less_500, ]
```

```{r}
top_k <- 15
all_level1 <- c("CD4", "CD8", "Treg")
structure_plots <- list()
for (level1_cat in all_level1) {
  cells_in_cat <- which(seurat_meta_filtered_no_thymocytes$annotation_level1 == level1_cat)
  L_pm_cat <- L_pm_no_thymocytes_normalized[cells_in_cat, ]
  seurat_meta_cat <- seurat_meta_filtered_no_thymocytes[cells_in_cat, ]
  
  level2_in_cat <- unique(seurat_meta_cat$annotation_level2)
  level2_in_cat <- level2_in_cat[order(level2_in_cat)]
  
  # match level2 categories to factors
  selected_level2 <- grep(paste0(level1_cat, "_"), rownames(level_2_AUC), value = TRUE)
  # Select the top_k in terms of AUC
  level2_category_to_factor <- colnames(level_2_AUC)[order(-apply(X = level_2_AUC[selected_level2,], FUN = max, MARGIN = 2))[1:top_k]]

  # filter out cells in selected level2 categories
  cells_selected_level2 <- which(seurat_meta_cat$annotation_level2 %in% selected_level2)
  L_pm_cat <- L_pm_cat[cells_selected_level2, ]
  seurat_meta_cat <- seurat_meta_cat[cells_selected_level2, ]
  
  fit_cat <- L_pm_cat[, level2_category_to_factor, drop = FALSE]
  cell_type_cat <- seurat_meta_cat$annotation_level2
  
  # re-level cell_type_cat to remove unused levels
  cell_type_cat <- factor(cell_type_cat, levels = (selected_level2))

  # Make sure all the level2 categories have at least 500 cells
  level2_counts <- table(cell_type_cat)
  level2_selected <- names(level2_counts[level2_counts >= 500])
  cells <- which(cell_type_cat %in% level2_selected)
  
  plot_new <- structure_plot(fit_cat[cells,], gap = 20,n = 5000,
               grouping = cell_type_cat[cells]) +
  labs(y = "membership",color = "",fill = "") +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(nrow = 1),
    color = guide_legend(nrow = 1)
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.box = "horizontal",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 13)
  )
    
    structure_plots[[paste0("p", which(all_level1 == level1_cat))]] <- plot_new
}

plot_grid(structure_plots$p1,
          structure_plots$p2,
          structure_plots$p3,
          nrow = 3,ncol = 1,
          rel_heights = c(4,4,4))


ggsave(
  filename = paste0("figures/Figure1/Figure1g_Structure_Level2.pdf"),
  width = 15, height = 15, dpi = 300,
  device = cairo_pdf
)
```



## (g) level2 boxplot

```{r}
num_truncated <- 15
factor_id_vec <- c(8, 13, 36, 93)
p_list <- list()
for (factor_id in factor_id_vec) {
  df_boxplot <- data.frame(Loading = L_pm_no_thymocytes_normalized[, factor_id], Level2  = seurat_meta_filtered_no_thymocytes$annotation_level2)
  
  level2_stats <- df_boxplot %>%
    group_by(Level2) %>%
    summarise(median_loading = median(Loading, na.rm = TRUE),
              .groups = "drop")
  
  top_level2 <- level2_stats %>%
    slice_max(median_loading, n = num_truncated, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    arrange(desc(median_loading))
  
  df_boxplot_top <- df_boxplot %>%
    filter(Level2 %in% top_level2$Level2) %>%
    mutate(Level2 = factor(Level2, levels = top_level2$Level2))
  
  p_new <- ggplot(df_boxplot_top, aes(x = Level2, y = Loading)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
    labs(title = paste0("Factor ", factor_id),
         x = "Level-2 Annotation",
         y = "Normalized Loading") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  p_list[[as.character(factor_id)]] <- p_new
}
p_combined <- wrap_plots(p_list, ncol = 2)
p_combined
ggsave(
  filename = "figures/Figure1/Figure1g_Boxplot_Level2.pdf",
  plot = p_combined,
  width = 12, height = 12, dpi = 400
)
```




## (h) level2 heatmap

```{r}
level_2_AUC_list <- readRDS(paste0(data_path, "level_2_within_level_1_AUC_list_figure.rds"))
level_2_AUC <- level_2_AUC_list$auc
```

```{r}
# Take out the level-2 categories with less than 500 cells
which_less_500 <- sapply(rownames(level_2_AUC), function(x){
  sum(seurat_meta$annotation_level2 == x) < 500
})
level_2_AUC_filtered <- level_2_AUC[!which_less_500, ]

# Take out factors that never achieve AUC > 0.9 for any level-2 category
factors_to_keep <- which(apply(level_2_AUC_filtered, 2, function
(x) any(x > 0.9)))
level_2_AUC_final <- level_2_AUC_filtered[, factors_to_keep]

# Take out level-2 categories that never achieve AUC > 0.9 for any factor
level2_to_keep <- which(apply(level_2_AUC_final, 1, function
(x) any(x > 0.9)))
level_2_AUC_final <- level_2_AUC_final[level2_to_keep, ]
```

Compute the median loading of each factor in each level-2 category

```{r}
# level_2_categories <- rownames(level_2_AUC_final)
# do not filter for now
level_2_categories <- unique(seurat_meta_filtered_no_thymocytes$annotation_level2)
# factors <- colnames(level_2_AUC_final)
factors <- paste0("K", 1:200)
median_loading_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(level_2_categories), ncol = length(factors))
rownames(median_loading_matrix) <- level_2_categories
colnames(median_loading_matrix) <- factors
for (i in seq_along(level_2_categories)) {
  level2_cat <- as.character(level_2_categories[i])
  cells_in_cat <- which(seurat_meta_filtered_no_thymocytes$annotation_level2 == level2_cat)
  
  for (j in seq_along(factors)) {
    factor_id <- factors[j]
    median_loading <- median(L_pm_no_thymocytes_normalized[cells_in_cat, factor_id], na.rm = TRUE)
    median_loading_matrix[level2_cat, factor_id] <- median_loading
  }
}
```


```{r}
# compute the reference median loading, which is the median loading of the factor across all the other level-2 categories in the same level-1 category
# or just take the median loading across all other cells.
reference_median_loading <- matrix(NA, nrow = nrow(median_loading_matrix), ncol = ncol(median_loading_matrix))
rownames(reference_median_loading) <- rownames(median_loading_matrix)
colnames(reference_median_loading) <- colnames(median_loading_matrix)

for (i in seq_along(level_2_categories)) {
  level2_cat <- as.character(level_2_categories[i])
  level1_cat <- seurat_meta$annotation_level1[which(seurat_meta$annotation_level2 == level2_cat)[1]]
  cells_in_other_level2 <- which(seurat_meta_filtered_no_thymocytes$annotation_level1 == level1_cat &
                                  seurat_meta_filtered_no_thymocytes$annotation_level2 != level2_cat)
  # cells_in_other_level2 <- which(seurat_meta_filtered_no_thymocytes$annotation_level2 != level2_cat)
  for (j in seq_along(factors)) {
    factor_id <- factors[j]
    ref_median_loading <- median(L_pm_no_thymocytes_normalized[cells_in_other_level2, factor_id], na.rm = TRUE)
    reference_median_loading[level2_cat, factor_id] <- ref_median_loading
  }
}
```

```{r}
global_median <- apply(L_pm_no_thymocytes_normalized[, factors, drop = FALSE], 2, median, na.rm = TRUE)

reference_median_loading <- matrix(
  rep(global_median, each = length(level_2_categories)),
  nrow = length(level_2_categories),
  byrow = FALSE,
  dimnames = list(level_2_categories, factors)
)
```


compute log2 fold-change matrix

```{r}
log2fc_matrix <- log2((median_loading_matrix) / (reference_median_loading))
```

```{r}
log2fc_cap <- pmax(pmin(log2fc_matrix, 5), -5)
factor_levels <- colnames(log2fc_cap)
factor_levels <- factor_levels[order(as.numeric(sub("^K", "", factor_levels)))]
level2_names <- rownames(log2fc_cap)
level1_prefix <- sub("_.*$", "", level2_names)
level2_levels <- level2_names[order(level1_prefix, level2_names)]
df_long <- as.data.frame(log2fc_cap) |>
  rownames_to_column(var = "Level2") |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = -Level2,
    names_to  = "Factor",
    values_to = "log2fc"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Factor = factor(Factor, levels = factor_levels),
    Level2 = factor(Level2, levels = level2_levels)
  )

p <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = Level2, y = Factor, fill = log2fc)) +
  geom_tile(color = "grey30", size = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#4575B4", mid = "white", high = "#D73027",
    midpoint = 0, oob = scales::squish, name = "log2 FC"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(
    title = "log2 fold-change (cluster vs others in same level-1)",
    x = "", y = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 7) +
  theme(
    panel.grid  = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 7),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7),
    plot.title  = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 7),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 7),
    axis.ticks   = element_blank()
  )

p

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/Figure1/Figure1h_Level2_HeatmapFull.pdf",
  plot = p,
  width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300
)
```

```{r}
log2fc_cap <- pmax(pmin(log2fc_matrix, 2), -2)
# ---- remove factors (gp) that never exceed |log2fc| > 1 across all Level2 ----
keep_factors <- apply(abs(log2fc_cap) > 1, 2, any, na.rm = TRUE)
log2fc_cap <- log2fc_cap[, keep_factors, drop = FALSE]

factor_levels <- colnames(log2fc_cap)
factor_levels <- factor_levels[order(as.numeric(sub("^K", "", factor_levels)))]
level2_names <- rownames(log2fc_cap)
level1_prefix <- sub("_.*$", "", level2_names)
level2_levels <- level2_names[order(level1_prefix, level2_names)]
df_long <- as.data.frame(log2fc_cap) |>
  rownames_to_column(var = "Level2") |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = -Level2,
    names_to  = "Factor",
    values_to = "log2fc"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    Factor = factor(Factor, levels = factor_levels),
    Level2 = factor(Level2, levels = level2_levels)
  )

p <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = Level2, y = Factor, fill = log2fc)) +
  geom_tile(color = "grey30", size = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#4575B4", mid = "white", high = "#D73027",
    midpoint = 0, oob = scales::squish, name = "log2 FC"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(
    title = "log2 fold-change (cluster vs others in same level-1)",
    x = "", y = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 7) +
  theme(
    panel.grid  = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 7),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7),
    plot.title  = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 7),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 7),
    axis.ticks   = element_blank()
  )

p

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/Figure1/Figure1h_Level2_Heatmap.pdf",
  plot = p,
  width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300
)
```

# Figure 2:

## (a) Relevance of TFs to GPs

```{r}
CD4_TF_list <- read.table(paste0(data_path, "/TF-list/CD4_tf_level2_AUC.txt"), header = TRUE)
CD8_TF_list <- read.table(paste0(data_path, "/TF-list/CD8_tf_level2_AUC.txt"), header = TRUE)
CD8aa_TF_list <- read.table(paste0(data_path, "/TF-list/CD8aa_tf_level2_AUC.txt"), header = TRUE)
gdT_TF_list <- read.table(paste0(data_path, "/TF-list/gdT_tf_level2_AUC.txt"), header = TRUE)
DN_TF_list <- read.table(paste0(data_path, "/TF-list/DN_tf_level2_AUC.txt"), header = TRUE)
DP_TF_list <- read.table(paste0(data_path, "/TF-list/DP_tf_level2_AUC.txt"), header = TRUE)
Treg_TF_list <- read.table(paste0(data_path, "/TF-list/Treg_tf_level2_AUC.txt"), header = TRUE)
non_conv_TF_list <- read.table(paste0(data_path, "/TF-list/nonconv_tf_level2_AUC.txt"), header = TRUE)
```

```{r}
threshold <- 0 # include all TFs
TF_highlights <- c(CD4_TF_list$Gene[CD4_TF_list$auc >= threshold],
                   CD8_TF_list$Gene[CD8_TF_list$auc >= threshold],
                   CD8aa_TF_list$Gene[CD8aa_TF_list$auc >= threshold],
                   gdT_TF_list$Gene[gdT_TF_list$auc >= threshold],
                   DN_TF_list$Gene[DN_TF_list$auc >= threshold],
                   DP_TF_list$Gene[DP_TF_list$auc >= threshold],
                   non_conv_TF_list$Gene[non_conv_TF_list$auc >= threshold],
                   Treg_TF_list$Gene[Treg_TF_list$auc >= threshold])

TF_highlights <- unique(TF_highlights)
```

```{r}
F_pm <- flashier_snmf_summary$F_pm
d_f <- apply(F_pm, 2, function(x) max(abs(x)))
F_pm_norm_col <- scale_cols(F_pm,1/d_f)

# strongly-replicated
factor.info <- read.csv(file = paste0(data_path, "factors_comments_updated.xlsx - factor_pve.csv"), header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
factors_considered <- sort(factor.info$X[factor.info$strongly.replicated])
```

Produce stacked barplot:

```{r}
produce_stacked_barplot_signed <- function(
  tfs,
  F_pm_norm_col,
  select_gp = NULL,         # indices or "Kxx"
  threshold = NULL,        
  label_step = NULL,
  cex_axis = 0.7,
  las = 2,
  legend_cex = 0.8,
  legend_ncol = NULL,
  palette = NULL,
  legend_pos = "topright",
  keep_order = TRUE
) {
  tfs <- intersect(tfs, rownames(F_pm_norm_col))
  if (!length(tfs)) stop("None of the tfs are in rownames(F_pm_norm_col).")

  # --- select GPs (keep original indices)
  K_all <- seq_len(ncol(F_pm_norm_col))
  if (!is.null(select_gp)) {
    idx <- if (is.character(select_gp)) as.integer(sub("^K", "", select_gp)) else as.integer(select_gp)
    idx <- idx[idx >= 1 & idx <= max(K_all)]
  } else idx <- K_all

  if (keep_order) idx <- sort(idx)

  mat <- F_pm_norm_col[tfs, idx, drop = FALSE]  # raw signed loadings

  # split into positive / negative magnitudes
  pos <- pmax(mat, 0)
  neg <- -pmin(mat, 0)  # magnitudes (positive numbers)

  # axis labels
  gp_labels <- paste0("K", idx)
  if (is.null(label_step)) {
    label_step <- if (length(idx) <= 40) 1 else if (length(idx) <= 80) 2 else 3
  }
  tick_i <- seq(1, length(idx), by = label_step)

  # colors
  if (is.null(palette)) {
    palette <- hcl.colors(length(tfs), palette = "Dark 3")
  } else {
    stopifnot(length(palette) >= length(tfs))
    palette <- palette[seq_along(tfs)]
  }

  # y-limits (adaptive)
  ymax <- max(colSums(pos, na.rm = TRUE))
  ymin <- max(colSums(neg, na.rm = TRUE))
  ylim <- c(-1.05 * max(1e-8, ymin), 1.05 * max(1e-8, ymax))

  # margins (space for x labels + legend)
  op <- par(mar = c(9, 4, 3, 6))

  # draw positive stacks
  bp <- barplot(
    pos,
    beside = FALSE,
    col = palette,
    names.arg = rep("", ncol(pos)),
    las = las,
    ylim = ylim,
    ylab = "normalized factor values",
    main = "TF relevance across GPs (signed)"
  )

  # draw negative stacks (as negative values)
  barplot(
    -neg,
    beside = FALSE,
    col = palette,
    names.arg = rep("", ncol(neg)),
    add = TRUE,
    axes = FALSE
  )

  # x-axis
  axis(1, at = bp[tick_i], labels = gp_labels[tick_i], las = las, cex.axis = cex_axis)
  abline(h = 0)

  # optional: mark GPs with any TF |loading| >= threshold
  if (!is.null(threshold)) {
    hit_gp <- apply(abs(mat), 2, function(x) any(x >= threshold, na.rm = TRUE))
    if (any(hit_gp)) {
      points(bp[hit_gp], rep(0, sum(hit_gp)), pch = 19) 
    }
  }

  # legend
  if (is.null(legend_ncol)) {
    legend_ncol <- ifelse(length(tfs) <= 10, 1, ifelse(length(tfs) <= 20, 2, 3))
  }
  legend(legend_pos, legend = tfs, fill = palette, cex = legend_cex,
         ncol = legend_ncol, bty = "n")

  par(op)
  invisible(list(gps = gp_labels, tfs = tfs, colors = palette))
}


pdf("figures/Figure2/Figure2a_TFs_GP.pdf", width = 10, height = 9)
produce_stacked_barplot_signed(
  c("Tcf7", "Rorc", "Zfp683", "Etv5", "Maf", "Myb", "Aff3", "Lef1", "Foxp3"),
  F_pm_norm_col,
  select_gp = factors_considered,
  label_step = 2,
  threshold = NULL  
)
dev.off()
```


## (b) Rank plot of TF

This figure shows, for each TF, the (n_genes - rank) across all GPs, where rank is determined by the (positive / absolute) loading of that TF in each GP. A higher (n_genes - rank) means that the TF is more specific to that GP.

```{r}
F_pm <- flashier_snmf_summary$F_pm
d_f <- apply(F_pm, 2, function(x) max(abs(x)))
F_pm_norm_col <- scale_cols(F_pm,1/d_f)

# strongly-replicated
factor.info <- read.csv(file = paste0(data_path, "factors_comments_updated.xlsx - factor_pve.csv"), header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
factors_considered <- sort(factor.info$X[factor.info$strongly.replicated])

colnames(F_pm_norm_col) <- paste0("K", seq_len(ncol(F_pm_norm_col)))
```

```{r}
# Compute TF rank (within each GEP/column) across all GEPs
.compute_tf_rank_score <- function(F, tf, mode = c("positive", "abs", "raw")) {
  mode <- match.arg(mode)

  if (is.null(rownames(F))) stop("F must have rownames (gene names).")
  if (!tf %in% rownames(F)) stop(sprintf("TF '%s' not found in rownames(F).", tf))

  G <- ncol(F)
  cols <- colnames(F)
  if (is.null(cols)) cols <- paste0("K", seq_len(G))

  score_mat <- switch(
    mode,
    positive = pmax(F, 0),
    abs      = abs(F),
    raw      = F
  )

  # Rank within each column: higher score -> smaller rank (rank=1 is best)
  rank_mat <- apply(score_mat, 2, function(x) rank(-x, ties.method = "average", na.last = "keep"))
  tf_rank <- as.numeric(rank_mat[tf, ])

  data.frame(
    GEP  = factor(cols, levels = cols),
    rank = tf_rank,
    tf   = tf,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

plot_tf_rank_bars <- function(
  F,
  TFs,
  mode = c("positive", "abs", "raw"),
  rank_max = 100,
  x_label_every = 10,
  facet = TRUE,
  alpha = 0.9,
  label_best = TRUE
) {
  mode <- match.arg(mode)

  df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(TFs, function(tf) .compute_tf_rank_score(F, tf, mode = mode)))

  # Ensure numeric rank; drop missing ranks
  df$rank <- as.numeric(df$rank)
  df <- df[is.finite(df$rank), , drop = FALSE]

  # Numeric x for dense categories (1..G)
  levs <- levels(df$GEP)
  df$x <- as.integer(df$GEP)

  # Truncate worse ranks to rank_max (so they become height 0)
  df$rank_clip <- pmin(df$rank, rank_max)

  # "Skyscraper" height: better rank (smaller) -> taller bar
  df$height <- rank_max - df$rank_clip

  # Show fewer x tick labels to avoid overlap
  keep_idx <- seq(1, length(levs), by = x_label_every)
  x_breaks <- keep_idx
  x_labels <- levs[keep_idx]

  # y-axis: display as rank (1 at top, rank_max at bottom) via relabeling
  y_breaks <- seq(0, rank_max, by = 25)
  y_labels <- rank_max - y_breaks
  # If you prefer 1 instead of 0 at the very top, you can tweak labels:
  # y_labels[ y_labels == 0 ] <- 1  # optional, but usually not needed.

  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = x, y = height, fill = tf)) +
    geom_col(alpha = alpha, width = 0.9) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = x_breaks, labels = x_labels) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      breaks = y_breaks,
      labels = y_labels,
      limits = c(0, rank_max)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = sprintf("TF rank bar skyline across GEPs (top %d)", rank_max),
      x = "GEP",
      y = "Rank within each GEP (1 = highest)"
    ) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(vjust = 0.5, hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = if (facet) "none" else "right",
      legend.title = element_blank()
    )

  if (facet) {
    p <- p + facet_wrap(~tf, ncol = 1, scales = "fixed")
  }

  # Label the best GEP per TF (smallest rank -> tallest bar)
  if (isTRUE(label_best) && nrow(df) > 0) {
    best_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(split(df, df$tf), function(d) d[which.min(d$rank), ]))
    best_df$label <- as.character(best_df$GEP)

    p <- p + geom_text(
      data = best_df,
      aes(x = x, y = height, label = label),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      vjust = -0.4,  # place label slightly above the bar
      size = 3
    )
  }

  p
}

p3 <- plot_tf_rank_bars(
  F = F_pm_norm_col,
  TFs = c("Tcf7", "Foxp3", "Rorc"),
  mode = "abs",
  rank_max = 100,
  x_label_every = 10,
  facet = TRUE,
  label_best = TRUE
)

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/Figure2/Figure2b_rank_TF_GP.pdf",
  plot = p3, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300
)
```

```{r}
save_all_tf_rank_plots_pdf <- function(
  F,
  TFs,
  out_pdf,
  mode = c("positive", "abs", "raw"),
  rank_max = 100,
  x_label_every = 10,
  alpha = 0.9,
  width = 10,
  height = 4,
  dpi = 300
) {
  mode <- match.arg(mode)

  # Use cairo if available (better text rendering)
  # If cairo is not available on your system, replace with pdf(out_pdf, ...)
  if (capabilities("cairo")) {
    grDevices::cairo_pdf(filename = out_pdf, width = width, height = height)
  } else {
    grDevices::pdf(file = out_pdf, width = width, height = height)
  }
  on.exit(grDevices::dev.off(), add = TRUE)

  for (tf in TFs) {
    if (!tf %in% rownames(F)) next

    p <- plot_tf_rank_bars(
      F = F,
      TFs = tf,               # one TF per page
      mode = mode,
      rank_max = rank_max,
      x_label_every = x_label_every,
      facet = FALSE,          # no facet when only one TF
      alpha = alpha,
      label_best = TRUE
    ) +
      ggtitle(sprintf("TF rank across GEPs: %s (top %d)", tf, rank_max))

    print(p)
  }

  invisible(out_pdf)
}

# save to a multi-page PDF (one TF per page)
save_all_tf_rank_plots_pdf(
  F = F_pm_norm_col,
  TFs = TF_highlights,
  out_pdf = "figures/Figure2/Figure2b_rank_TF_GP_allTFs.pdf",
  mode = "abs",
  rank_max = 100,
  x_label_every = 10,
  width = 10,
  height = 4
)
```

```{r}
compute_tf_rank_matrix <- function(
  F,
  TFs,
  mode = c("positive", "abs", "raw"),
  ties.method = "average",
  verbose = TRUE
) {
  mode <- match.arg(mode)

  if (is.null(rownames(F))) stop("F must have rownames (gene names).")
  if (is.null(colnames(F))) colnames(F) <- paste0("K", seq_len(ncol(F)))

  TFs_in <- intersect(TFs, rownames(F))
  TFs_drop <- setdiff(TFs, TFs_in)

  if (verbose && length(TFs_drop) > 0) {
    message(sprintf(
      "Dropping %d TFs not found in rownames(F). Example: %s",
      length(TFs_drop),
      paste(head(TFs_drop, 5), collapse = ", ")
    ))
  }
  if (length(TFs_in) == 0) stop("None of the requested TFs are present in rownames(F).")

  score_mat <- switch(
    mode,
    positive = pmax(F, 0),
    abs      = abs(F),
    raw      = F
  )

  # rank within each column (GP): higher score => smaller rank (1 = best)
  rank_all <- apply(score_mat, 2, function(x) rank(-x, ties.method = ties.method, na.last = "keep"))

  # subset rows to TFs
  rank_tf <- rank_all[TFs_in, , drop = FALSE]

  list(
    rank = rank_tf,       # matrix: TF x GP
    TFs_used = TFs_in,
    TFs_dropped = TFs_drop
  )
}

rk_obj <- compute_tf_rank_matrix(F_pm_norm_col, TF_highlights, mode = "abs")
rank_tf <- rk_obj$rank

library(pheatmap)
rank_max <- 100
rank_clip <- pmin(rank_tf, rank_max)
keep <- apply(rank_clip, 1, function(v) any(v < rank_max, na.rm = TRUE))
rank_clip <- rank_clip[keep, , drop = FALSE]
pal <- colorRampPalette(c("red", "white"))(100)
bk  <- seq(1, rank_max, length.out = length(pal) + 1)

pheatmap(
  mat = rank_clip,
  color = pal,
  breaks = bk,
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,          
  show_colnames = TRUE,         
  fontsize_row = 6,
  main = sprintf("TF × GP rank heatmap (abs; ranks 1–%d)", rank_max),
  border_color = "grey85",
  legend_breaks = seq(1, rank_max, by = 10),
  legend_labels = as.character(seq(1, rank_max, by = 10))
)
```

This time just look at the TF factors:

```{r}
F_pm_TF <- F_pm_norm_col[rownames(F_pm_norm_col) %in% TF_highlights, ]

# only include TFs with at least one abs factor > 0.3
keep <- apply(abs(F_pm_TF), 1, function(v) any(v > 0.3, na.rm = TRUE))
F_pm_TF <- F_pm_TF[keep, , drop = FALSE]

# only include GPs with at least one TF abs factor > 0.2
keep_gp <- apply(abs(F_pm_TF), 2, function(v) any(v > 0.2, na.rm = TRUE))
F_pm_TF <- F_pm_TF[, keep_gp, drop = FALSE]

pdf("figures/Figure2/Figure2b_TF_GP_heatmap.pdf", width = 12, height = 10)
pheatmap(
  (F_pm_TF),
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = TRUE,
  fontsize_row = 6,
  fontsize_col = 8,
  main = "TF × GP loading heatmap (normalized; TFs only)",
  legend = TRUE
)
dev.off()
```




## (c) Relevance of markers to GPs

Define a few set of markers by threshold logFC in each GP:

```{r}
protein_flash_summary_lognorm <- readRDS(file = paste0(data_path, "protein_flash_selected_summary_lognorm.rds"))
# normalize by the max(abs) of each column
D_lognorm <- diag(1 / apply(protein_flash_summary_lognorm$F_pm, 2, function(x) max(abs(x))))
Protein_F_pm <- protein_flash_summary_lognorm$F_pm %*% D_lognorm
factor.info <- read.csv(file = paste0(data_path, "factors_comments_updated.xlsx - factor_pve.csv"), header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
factors_considered <- sort(factor.info$X[factor.info$strongly.replicated])
colnames(Protein_F_pm) <- paste0("K", factors_considered)
```

Take out poor quality proteins and proteins with name starting with Isotype

```{r}
isotype_proteins <- grep("^Isotype", rownames(Protein_F_pm), value = TRUE)
# TableS4_citeseq_qc_20250513.csv
proteins_quality <- read.csv(paste0(data_path, "TableS4_citeseq_qc_20250513.csv"), header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, skip = 1)
poor_proteins <- proteins_quality$protein[proteins_quality$classification == "poor"]
Protein_F_pm <- Protein_F_pm[!rownames(Protein_F_pm) %in% c(isotype_proteins, poor_proteins), ]

# redo the normalization
D_lognorm <- diag(1 / apply(Protein_F_pm, 2, function(x) max(abs(x))))
Protein_F_pm <- Protein_F_pm %*% D_lognorm
colnames(Protein_F_pm) <- paste0("K", factors_considered)
```

```{r}
produce_stacked_barplot_signed_citeseq <- function(
  tfs,
  F_pm_norm_col,
  threshold = NULL,        
  label_step = NULL,
  cex_axis = 0.7,
  las = 2,
  legend_cex = 0.8,
  legend_ncol = NULL,
  palette = NULL,
  legend_pos = "bottomright"
) {
  tfs <- intersect(tfs, rownames(F_pm_norm_col))
  if (!length(tfs)) stop("None of the tfs are in rownames(F_pm_norm_col).")

  mat <- F_pm_norm_col[tfs, , drop = FALSE]  # raw signed loadings

  # split into positive / negative magnitudes
  pos <- pmax(mat, 0)
  neg <- -pmin(mat, 0)  # magnitudes (positive numbers)

  # axis labels
  gp_labels <- colnames(F_pm_norm_col)
  idx <- seq_len(ncol(F_pm_norm_col))
  tick_i <- seq(1, length(idx), by = label_step)

  # colors
  if (is.null(palette)) {
    palette <- hcl.colors(length(tfs), palette = "Dark 3")
  } else {
    stopifnot(length(palette) >= length(tfs))
    palette <- palette[seq_along(tfs)]
  }

  # y-limits (adaptive)
  ymax <- max(colSums(pos, na.rm = TRUE))
  ymin <- max(colSums(neg, na.rm = TRUE))
  ylim <- c(-1.05 * max(1e-8, ymin), 1.05 * max(1e-8, ymax))

  # margins (space for x labels + legend)
  op <- par(mar = c(9, 4, 3, 6))

  # draw positive stacks
  bp <- barplot(
    pos,
    beside = FALSE,
    col = palette,
    names.arg = rep("", ncol(pos)),
    las = las,
    ylim = ylim,
    ylab = "normalized factors",
    main = "Markers relevance across GPs (signed)"
  )

  # draw negative stacks (as negative values)
  barplot(
    -neg,
    beside = FALSE,
    col = palette,
    names.arg = rep("", ncol(neg)),
    add = TRUE,
    axes = FALSE
  )

  # x-axis
  axis(1, at = bp[tick_i], labels = gp_labels[tick_i], las = las, cex.axis = cex_axis)
  abline(h = 0)

  # optional: mark GPs with any TF |loading| >= threshold
  if (!is.null(threshold)) {
    hit_gp <- apply(abs(mat), 2, function(x) any(x >= threshold, na.rm = TRUE))
    if (any(hit_gp)) {
      points(bp[hit_gp], rep(0, sum(hit_gp)), pch = 19) 
    }
  }

  # legend
  if (is.null(legend_ncol)) {
    legend_ncol <- ifelse(length(tfs) <= 10, 1, ifelse(length(tfs) <= 20, 2, 3))
  }
  legend(legend_pos, legend = tfs, fill = palette, cex = legend_cex,
         ncol = legend_ncol, bty = "n")

  par(op)
  invisible(list(gps = gp_labels, tfs = tfs, colors = palette))
}

# produce stacked barplot for markers
pdf("figures/Figure2/Figure2c_Markers_GP.pdf", width = 10, height = 9)
produce_stacked_barplot_signed_citeseq(
  c("CD4", "IL2RA.CD25", "FR4", "CD8A", "CD8B"),
  Protein_F_pm,
  label_step = 2,
  threshold = NULL  
)
dev.off()
```


## (d) Enrichment of markers in GPs

Define set of markers for each GP based on threshold:

```{r}
marker_threshold <- 0.5
gp_marker_list <- list()
for (i in 1:ncol(Protein_F_pm)) {
  gp_name <- colnames(Protein_F_pm)[i]
  marker_genes <- rownames(Protein_F_pm)[which(abs(Protein_F_pm)[, i] >= marker_threshold)]
  gp_marker_list[[gp_name]] <- marker_genes
}

gp_marker_sign_list <- list()
for (i in 1:ncol(Protein_F_pm)) {
  gp_name <- colnames(Protein_F_pm)[i]
  marker_genes_pos <- rownames(Protein_F_pm)[which(Protein_F_pm[, i] >= marker_threshold)]
  marker_genes_neg <- rownames(Protein_F_pm)[which(Protein_F_pm[, i] <= -marker_threshold)]
  gp_marker_sign_list[[gp_name]] <- list(
    pos = marker_genes_pos,
    neg = marker_genes_neg
  )
}
```

```{r}
make_ucell_features <- function(gp_marker_sign_list) {
  feats <- lapply(gp_marker_sign_list, function(x) {
    pos <- x$pos
    neg <- x$neg
    
    c(
      if (length(pos)) paste0(pos, "+"),
      if (length(neg)) paste0(neg, "-")
    )
  })
  
  feats <- feats[vapply(feats, length, integer(1)) > 0]
  
  feats <- lapply(feats, unique)
  feats
}
features_ucell <- make_ucell_features(gp_marker_sign_list)
```

How many markers per GP?

```{r}
sapply(features_ucell, length)
```

If there are too many protein markers (>10) in that GP, we view the protein marker set from that GP as "non-informative", and skip that set.

```{r}
marker_threshold <- 0.5
gp_marker_list <- list()
gp_marker_sign_list <- list()
for (i in 1:ncol(Protein_F_pm)) {
  gp_name <- colnames(Protein_F_pm)[i]
  marker_values <- Protein_F_pm[, i]
  marker_genes <- rownames(Protein_F_pm)[which(abs(marker_values) >= marker_threshold)]
  
  if (length(marker_genes) > 10){
    next
  }
  
  gp_marker_list[[gp_name]] <- marker_genes
  
  marker_genes_pos <- marker_genes[which(marker_values[marker_genes] >= marker_threshold)]
  marker_genes_neg <- marker_genes[which(marker_values[marker_genes] <= -marker_threshold)]
  gp_marker_sign_list[[gp_name]] <- list(
    pos = marker_genes_pos,
    neg = marker_genes_neg
  )
}

features_ucell <- make_ucell_features(gp_marker_sign_list)
```

Treat neg and pos in different ways:

```{r}
library(UCell)

Knames <- colnames(Protein_F_pm)
names(gp_marker_sign_list) <- paste0("Set_", names(gp_marker_sign_list))
names(gp_marker_list) <- paste0("Set_", names(gp_marker_list))
Set_names <- names(gp_marker_sign_list)

custom_score <- matrix(NA_real_,
                       nrow = length(Knames),
                       ncol = length(Set_names),
                       dimnames = list(Knames, Set_names))

for (set_name in Set_names) {
  markers_neg <- gp_marker_sign_list[[set_name]]$neg

  Protein_F_pm_signed <- Protein_F_pm
  if (length(markers_neg) > 0) {
    Protein_F_pm_signed[markers_neg, ] <- -Protein_F_pm_signed[markers_neg, ]
  }

  out <- ScoreSignatures_UCell(
    matrix   = as.matrix(Protein_F_pm_signed),
    features = gp_marker_list[set_name],   
    maxRank  = nrow(Protein_F_pm_signed)
  )

  custom_score[, set_name] <- out  
}
```

```{r}
make_sorted_curve_ms <- function(v, mode = c("positive", "abs"), transform = c("none","log1p")) {
  mode <- match.arg(mode)
  transform <- match.arg(transform)

  score <- switch(mode,
                  positive = pmax(v, 0),
                  abs      = abs(v))

  ord <- order(score, decreasing = TRUE, na.last = TRUE)
  s   <- score[ord]

  y <- switch(transform,
              none  = s,
              log1p = log1p(s))

  res <- data.frame(
    rank = seq_along(s),
    y    = y,
    score = s,
    gp   = names(v)[ord]
  )
  res
}

plot_sorted_marker_enrichment <- function(
  custom_score,
  marker_highlights,
  mode = "positive", # doesn't matter since scores are non-negative
  transform = "none",        # "none" or "log1p"
  n_bg = 80,
  seed = 1,
  label_at = 0.95,
  title = "Sorted marker-set enrichment across GPs",
  ylab = NULL,
  ylim = c(0.5, 1),
  xlim = c(0, 40),
  show_points = TRUE,
  point_size = 0.7,
  point_alpha = 0.6,
  show_legend = TRUE,
  legend_pos = c(0.98, 0.98) # inside-plot coordinates; top-right
) {
  M <- as.matrix(custom_score)
  stopifnot(!is.null(rownames(M)), !is.null(colnames(M)))

  all_sets <- colnames(M)
  ms_use   <- intersect(marker_highlights, all_sets)

  set.seed(seed)
  bg_sets <- setdiff(all_sets, ms_use)
  if (length(bg_sets) > n_bg) bg_sets <- sample(bg_sets, n_bg)

  bg_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(bg_sets, function(ms) {
    v <- M[, ms]; names(v) <- rownames(M)
    d <- make_sorted_curve_ms(v, mode = mode, transform = transform)
    d$marker_set <- ms
    d$type <- "bg"
    d
  }))

  ms_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(ms_use, function(ms) {
    v <- M[, ms]; names(v) <- rownames(M)
    d <- make_sorted_curve_ms(v, mode = mode, transform = transform)
    d$marker_set <- ms
    d$type <- "ms"
    d
  }))

  lab_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(ms_use, function(ms) {
    d <- ms_df[ms_df$marker_set == ms, ]
    if (nrow(d) == 0) return(NULL)
    i <- max(1, min(nrow(d), round(label_at * nrow(d))))
    d[i, c("rank","y","marker_set")]
  }))

  if (is.null(ylab)) {
    ylab <- if (transform == "log1p") "log(1 + enrichment score)" else "Enrichment score"
  }

  p <- ggplot() +
    geom_line(data = bg_df, aes(rank, y, group = marker_set),
              linewidth = 0.4, alpha = 0.12) +
    geom_line(data = ms_df, aes(rank, y, color = marker_set,
                                linetype = marker_set, group = marker_set),
              linewidth = 0.75)

  if (isTRUE(show_points)) {
    p <- p +
      geom_point(data = bg_df, aes(rank, y, group = marker_set),
                 size = point_size, alpha = 0.08) +
      geom_point(data = ms_df, aes(rank, y, color = marker_set, group = marker_set),
                 size = point_size, alpha = point_alpha)
  }

  p <- p +
    labs(title = title,
         x = "Ranked GPs (by enrichment score)",
         y = ylab,
         color = NULL, linetype = NULL) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 12)

  if (isTRUE(show_legend)) {
    p <- p +
      theme(
        legend.position = legend_pos,
        legend.justification = c(1, 1),
        legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "grey70", linewidth = 0.2),
        legend.key.size = grid::unit(0.35, "cm")
      )
  } else {
    p <- p + theme(legend.position = "none")
  }

  if (!is.null(lab_df) && nrow(lab_df) > 0) {
    p <- p + geom_label_repel(
      data = lab_df,
      aes(rank, y, label = marker_set, color = marker_set),
      size = 3,
      label.size = 0.2,
      show.legend = FALSE,
      max.overlaps = Inf
    )
  }
  
  p <- p + coord_cartesian(ylim = ylim, xlim = xlim)

  p
}

# Example
p_sorted <- plot_sorted_marker_enrichment(
  custom_score,
  marker_highlights = c("Set_K2", "Set_K8", "Set_K22", "Set_K26", "Set_K54", "Set_K58", "Set_K68", "Set_K171", "Set_K182"),
  mode = "positive",
  transform = "none",   # try "log1p" if most scores are tiny
  n_bg = 30
)
p_sorted

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/Figure2/Figure2d_sorted_marker_enrichment.pdf",
  plot = p_sorted, width = 6, height = 6, dpi = 300
)
```

Produce a table of marker set for each GP:

```{r}
# ---- Convert gp_marker_sign_list to a LaTeX-friendly table ----
# gp_marker_sign_list: a named list like $Set_K2$pos / $Set_K2$neg
# Each row = one set; columns = Positive markers / Negative markers
marker_list_to_df <- function(gp_marker_sign_list, collapse = ", ") {
  stopifnot(is.list(gp_marker_sign_list), length(gp_marker_sign_list) > 0)

  sets <- names(gp_marker_sign_list)
  if (is.null(sets)) stop("gp_marker_sign_list must have names, e.g. 'Set_K68'.")

  df <- data.frame(
    Set = sets,
    Positive = vapply(gp_marker_sign_list, function(x) {
      pos <- x$pos
      if (length(pos) == 0) return("")
      paste(pos, collapse = collapse)
    }, character(1)),
    Negative = vapply(gp_marker_sign_list, function(x) {
      neg <- x$neg
      if (length(neg) == 0) return("")
      paste(neg, collapse = collapse)
    }, character(1)),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  df
}

# Build the table data
df_markers <- marker_list_to_df(gp_marker_sign_list)

# Optional: sort by set index (K2, K5, K6, ...)
df_markers$K_idx <- as.integer(sub(".*_K", "", df_markers$Set))
df_markers <- df_markers[order(df_markers$K_idx), c("Set", "Positive", "Negative")]

# ---- Output as LaTeX ----
# Option A: knitr::kable (simple and works well)
library(knitr)
df_markers <- df_markers[, c("Positive", "Negative")]
# If you use booktabs in your LaTeX preamble, set booktabs=TRUE
latex_code <- knitr::kable(
  df_markers,
  format = "latex",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  longtable = TRUE,     # good if the table is long
  escape = TRUE,        # keep TRUE unless you intentionally add LaTeX in cells
  caption = "Positive and negative protein markers for each marker set."
)

cat(latex_code)

```

```{r}
library(kableExtra)
tab <- knitr::kable(df_markers, format = "html", caption = "Positive and negative protein markers for each marker set.") |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)

html_file <- "tmp_table/table_preview.html"
png_file  <- "tmp_table/table_preview.png"
dir.create("tmp_table", showWarnings = FALSE)
kableExtra::save_kable(tab, file = html_file)
webshot2::webshot(html_file, file = png_file, zoom = 2)
png_file
```

Plot the maximal difference within the top 2 GPs for each marker set, against the number of markers in that set:

```{r}
max_diff_vs_nmarkers <- function(custom_score, gp_marker_list, top_n = 5) {
  stopifnot(is.matrix(custom_score), nrow(custom_score) >= 1,
            ncol(custom_score) >= 1)
  stopifnot(is.list(gp_marker_list), length(gp_marker_list) >= 1)
  sets <- colnames(custom_score)
  stopifnot(all(names(gp_marker_list) %in% sets))
  out <- data.frame(
    Set = character(0),
    n_markers = integer(0),
    max_diff = numeric(0),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  for (set in names(gp_marker_list)) {
    scores <- custom_score[, set]
    ord <- order(scores, decreasing = TRUE)
    top_scores <- scores[ord[1: min(top_n, length(ord))]]
    if (length(top_scores) >= 2) {
      max_diff <- max(top_scores) - min(top_scores)
    } else {
      max_diff <- NA_real_
    }
    n_markers <- length(gp_marker_list[[set]])
    out <- rbind(out, data.frame(
      Set = set,
      n_markers = n_markers,
      max_diff = max_diff,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
  }
  out
}
df_diff <- max_diff_vs_nmarkers(custom_score, gp_marker_list, top_n = 2)
p_diff <- ggplot(df_diff, aes(x = n_markers, y = max_diff, label = Set)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "Number of markers in set",
    y = "Difference in enrichment score (top 2 GPs)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)
# add lm line
p_diff <- p_diff + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "blue", linetype = "dashed")
p_diff
ggsave(
  filename = "figures/Figure2/Figure2d_maxDiff_vs_nMarkers.pdf",
  plot = p_diff, width = 6, height = 5, dpi = 300
)
```

Check a correlation heatmap of protein programs:

```{r}
# pheatmap of protein programs
library(pheatmap)
# make it sparse
Protein_F_pm_sparse <- ifelse(
  abs(Protein_F_pm) >= 0.5,
  Protein_F_pm,
  0
)
pdf("figures/Figure2/Figure2d_Protein_Programs_heatmap.pdf", width = 12, height = 10)
pheatmap::pheatmap(
  t(Protein_F_pm_sparse),
  main = "Protein programs (normalized loadings)"
)
dev.off()
```

```{r}
cor_protein_programs <- cor(Protein_F_pm, method = "pearson")
cor_protein_programs_threshold <- 0
cor_protein_programs_sparse <- ifelse(
  abs(cor_protein_programs) >= cor_protein_programs_threshold,
  cor_protein_programs,
  0
)
pdf("figures/Figure2/Figure2d_Protein_Programs_correlation_heatmap.pdf", width = 10, height = 10)
pheatmap::pheatmap(
  cor_protein_programs_sparse,
  main = "Correlation among protein programs"
)
dev.off()
```

Let's try to define protein-marker sets based on cluster of programs:

```{r}
ph <- pheatmap(
  cor_protein_programs,
  main = "Correlation among protein programs"
)

hc_programs <- ph$tree_col
prog_clusters <- cutree(hc_programs, h = 2)
table(prog_clusters)
```

Now compute the average profile within each cluster, and then obtain a new protein-marker set for each cluster:

```{r}
gp_marker_sign_list_clustered <- list()
gp_marker_list_clustered <- list()
included_progs <- list()
for (clust_id in unique(prog_clusters)) {
  prog_in_clust <- names(prog_clusters)[prog_clusters == clust_id]
  avg_profile <- rowMeans(Protein_F_pm[, prog_in_clust, drop = FALSE])
  included_progs[[paste0("Cluster_", clust_id)]] <- prog_in_clust
  marker_genes <- names(avg_profile)[which(abs(avg_profile) >= marker_threshold)]
  gp_marker_list_clustered[[paste0("Cluster_", clust_id)]] <- marker_genes
  
  marker_genes_pos <- marker_genes[which(avg_profile[marker_genes] >= marker_threshold)]
  marker_genes_neg <- marker_genes[which(avg_profile[marker_genes] <= -marker_threshold)]
  gp_marker_sign_list_clustered[[paste0("Cluster_", clust_id)]] <- list(
    pos = marker_genes_pos,
    neg = marker_genes_neg
  )
}
```

Redo the table for clustered set:

```{r}
# Build the table data
df_markers_clustered <- marker_list_to_df(gp_marker_sign_list_clustered)
df_markers_clustered <- df_markers_clustered[order(df_markers_clustered$Set), c("Set", "Positive", "Negative")]
df_markers_clustered <- df_markers_clustered[, c("Positive", "Negative")]

# include what program each cluster corresponds to
df_markers_clustered$programs <- vapply(rownames(df_markers_clustered), function(clust_name) {
  progs <- included_progs[[clust_name]]
  paste(progs, collapse = ", ")
}, character(1))
```

```{r}
library(kableExtra)
tab2 <- knitr::kable(df_markers_clustered, format = "html", caption = "Positive and negative protein markers for each marker set.") |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)

html_file <- "tmp_table/table_preview_clust.html"
png_file  <- "tmp_table/table_preview_clust.png"
dir.create("tmp_table", showWarnings = FALSE)
kableExtra::save_kable(tab2, file = html_file)
webshot2::webshot(html_file, file = png_file, zoom = 2)
png_file
```

Look at the enrichment table for these clustered sets:

```{r}
custom_score_clustered <- matrix(NA_real_,
                       nrow = length(Knames),
                       ncol = length(gp_marker_sign_list_clustered),
                       dimnames = list(Knames, names(gp_marker_sign_list_clustered)))

for (set_name in names(gp_marker_sign_list_clustered)) {
  markers_neg <- gp_marker_sign_list_clustered[[set_name]]$neg

  Protein_F_pm_signed <- Protein_F_pm
  if (length(markers_neg) > 0) {
    Protein_F_pm_signed[markers_neg, ] <- -Protein_F_pm_signed[markers_neg, ]
  }

  out <- ScoreSignatures_UCell(
    matrix   = as.matrix(Protein_F_pm_signed),
    features = gp_marker_list_clustered[set_name],
    maxRank  = nrow(Protein_F_pm_signed)
  )

  custom_score_clustered[, set_name] <- out  
}
```

```{r}
p_sorted_clust <- plot_sorted_marker_enrichment(
  custom_score_clustered,
  marker_highlights = names(gp_marker_sign_list_clustered)[c(1,6,7,9,23,28)],
  mode = "positive",
  transform = "none",   # try "log1p" if most scores are tiny
  n_bg = 15
)
ggsave(
  filename = "figures/Figure2/Figure2d_sorted_marker_enrichment_clust.pdf",
  plot = p_sorted_clust, width = 6, height = 6, dpi = 300
)
```



# Figure 3:

## (a) GP correlation among level-1 categories

```{r}
make_gp_pair_cor_table <- function(
  L_pm,
  meta,
  cell_col = "annotation_level1",
  gp_use = NULL,          # NULL -> use all colnames(L_pm)
  pairs = NULL,           # NULL -> all pairs among gp_use; or c("K3-K10","K1-K4")
  method = "pearson",
  min_cells = 30L,
  top_pairs = NULL        # optional: keep top N pairs by mean |cor| across cell types
) {
  stopifnot(nrow(L_pm) == nrow(meta))

  # Ensure GP names
  if (is.null(colnames(L_pm))) colnames(L_pm) <- paste0("K", seq_len(ncol(L_pm)))

  if (is.null(gp_use)) gp_use <- colnames(L_pm)
  gp_use <- intersect(gp_use, colnames(L_pm))
  stopifnot(length(gp_use) >= 2)

  # Build/parse pairs
  if (is.null(pairs)) {
    cmb <- utils::combn(gp_use, 2)
    pairs <- paste0(cmb[1, ], "-", cmb[2, ])
  } else {
    pairs <- as.character(pairs)
  }
  gp1 <- sub("-.*$", "", pairs)
  gp2 <- sub("^.*-", "", pairs)
  pair_df <- data.frame(pair = pairs, gp1 = gp1, gp2 = gp2, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

  # Cell types
  cell_types <- sort(unique(as.character(meta[[cell_col]])))
  cell_types <- cell_types[!is.na(cell_types)]

  out <- vector("list", length(cell_types))
  names(out) <- cell_types

  for (ct in cell_types) {
    idx <- meta[[cell_col]] == ct
    idx[is.na(idx)] <- FALSE
    if (sum(idx) < min_cells) next

    C <- stats::cor(L_pm[idx, gp_use, drop = FALSE],
                    method = method, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

    keep <- pair_df$gp1 %in% colnames(C) & pair_df$gp2 %in% colnames(C)
    tmp <- pair_df[keep, , drop = FALSE]
    tmp$cell_type <- ct
    tmp$cor <- C[cbind(tmp$gp1, tmp$gp2)]
    tmp$n_cells <- sum(idx)

    out[[ct]] <- tmp[, c("pair","gp1","gp2","cell_type","n_cells","cor")]
  }

  tab <- do.call(rbind, out)
  tab <- tab[!is.na(tab$cor), , drop = FALSE]

  # Optionally keep only top pairs by mean |cor|
  if (!is.null(top_pairs)) {
    agg <- aggregate(abs(cor) ~ pair, data = tab, FUN = mean)
    keep_pairs <- head(agg[order(agg[[2]], decreasing = TRUE), "pair"], top_pairs)
    tab <- tab[tab$pair %in% keep_pairs, , drop = FALSE]
  }

  tab
}

plot_gp_pair_cor_heatmap <- function(
  tab,
  pair_order = c("mean_abs", "none"),
  cell_order = c("alphabetical", "none"),
  limits = c(-1, 1)
) {
  pair_order <- match.arg(pair_order)
  cell_order <- match.arg(cell_order)

  df <- tab

  # Order pairs
  if (pair_order == "mean_abs") {
    ord <- aggregate(abs(cor) ~ pair, data = df, FUN = mean)
    ord <- ord[order(ord[[2]], decreasing = TRUE), "pair"]
    df$pair <- factor(df$pair, levels = ord)
  } else {
    df$pair <- factor(df$pair, levels = unique(df$pair))
  }

  # Order cell types
  if (cell_order == "alphabetical") {
    df$cell_type <- factor(df$cell_type, levels = sort(unique(df$cell_type)))
  } else {
    df$cell_type <- factor(df$cell_type, levels = unique(df$cell_type))
  }

  ggplot2::ggplot(df, ggplot2::aes(x = cell_type, y = pair, fill = cor)) +
    ggplot2::geom_tile() +
    ggplot2::scale_fill_gradient2(limits = limits) +
    ggplot2::labs(x = "Cell type", y = "GP pair", fill = "cor") +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    ggplot2::theme(axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
}
```

```{r}
tab <- make_gp_pair_cor_table(
  L_pm = L_pm,
  meta = seurat_meta,
  cell_col = "annotation_level1",
  top_pairs = NULL
)

# remove thymocytes from tab
tab_non_thymocytes <- tab %>% filter(cell_type != "thymocyte")
```

```{r}
# strongly-replicated GPs
factor.info <- read.csv(file = paste0(data_path, "factors_comments_updated.xlsx - factor_pve.csv"), header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
factors_considered <- sort(factor.info$X[factor.info$strongly.replicated])
strongly_replicated_gps <- paste0("K", factors_considered)
```

```{r}
# only keep pairs where both GPs are strongly-replicated
tab_non_thymocytes <- tab_non_thymocytes %>%
  filter(gp1 %in% strongly_replicated_gps & gp2 %in% strongly_replicated_gps)

# only keep pairs with |cor| > 0.5 in at least one cell type
tab_non_thymocytes <- tab_non_thymocytes %>%
  group_by(pair) %>%
  filter(any(abs(cor) > 0.5)) %>%
  ungroup()

# only keep pairs with the range of cor larger than 0.1 across cell types
tab_non_thymocytes <- tab_non_thymocytes %>%
  group_by(pair) %>%
  filter((max(cor) - min(cor)) > 0.1) %>%
  ungroup()

# only keep the top 50 pairs in terms of range of cor across cell types
pair_ranges <- tab_non_thymocytes %>%
  group_by(pair) %>%
  summarize(cor_range = max(cor) - min(cor)) %>%
  arrange(desc(cor_range))

top_pairs <- head(pair_ranges$pair, 50)

tab_non_thymocytes <- tab_non_thymocytes %>%
  filter(pair %in% top_pairs)

p <- plot_gp_pair_cor_heatmap(tab_non_thymocytes)
p

ggsave(
  filename = "figures/Figure3/Figure3a_GP_pair_correlation_heatmap.pdf",
  plot = p, width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 300
)
```




# Supplementary Figures:

## Figure S1: Citeseq volcano plots

```{r}
protein_mat_normalized_lognorm <- readRDS(file = paste0(data_path, "protein_mat_normalized_lognorm.rds"))
protein_flash_summary_lognorm <- readRDS(file = paste0(data_path, "protein_flash_selected_summary_lognorm.rds"))
citeseq_F <- protein_flash_summary_lognorm$F_pm

factor.info <- read.csv(
  file = paste0(data_path, "factors_comments_updated.xlsx - factor_pve.csv"), 
  header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
factors_considered <- sort(factor.info$X[factor.info$strongly.replicated])
colnames(citeseq_F) <- paste0("F", factors_considered)
```

```{r}
mean_shifted_log_expr <- apply(protein_mat_normalized_lognorm, 2, function(x) mean(x))
plot_scatterplot <- function(factor_index, n_higlight = 10) {
  f_values <- citeseq_F[, factor_index]
  
  # normliaze f_values to have max absolute value 1
  f_values <- f_values / max(abs(f_values))
  
  gene_names <- rownames(citeseq_F)
  
  df <- data.frame(
    Gene = gene_names,
    FactorValue = f_values,
    MeanLogExpr = mean_shifted_log_expr[gene_names]
  )
  
  # plot FactorValue (x) vs MeanLogExpr (y)
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = FactorValue, y = MeanLogExpr))
  p <- p + geom_point(alpha = 0.5)
  p <- p + labs(
    title = colnames(citeseq_F)[factor_index],
    x = "Increase in Shifted Log Expression",
    y = "Mean Shifted Log Expression"
  ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
  # highlight top n_higlight genes with highest absolute factor values
  top_genes <- df %>%
    slice_max(order_by = abs(FactorValue), n = n_higlight)
  p <- p + geom_point(data = top_genes, aes(x = FactorValue, y = MeanLogExpr), color = "skyblue")
  p <- p + geom_text_repel(
    data = top_genes,
    aes(label = Gene),
    color = "skyblue",
    box.padding = 0.35, point.padding = 0.15,
    max.time = 1, max.iter = 5e3
  )
  return(p)
}
```

```{r}
# store all scatterplots in a list into one PDF
```
